title: Find Cycle Start Node
description:
Find If a cycle exists in a singly linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If no cycle exists, return None. Use O(1) space.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- Values arbitrary 32-bit
explanation:
Intuition
After slow and fast meet inside the cycle, resetting one pointer to head and advancing both one step at a time leads them to meet at the cycle entry. This follows from equalizing distances traveled.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Run Floyd to detect a meeting. If none, return None.
2. Main Algorithm: Reset slow to head and move both one step until they meet.
3. Return Result: Return the meeting node.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n) due to a single pass or divide-and-conquer
  over the list where each node is visited a bounded number of times.
- Space Complexity: O(1) accounting for pointers and, when needed,
  recursion depth or extra structures.

Key Insights
- Mathematical relation between path lengths yields the entry
- No additional memory is needed beyond pointers

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to handle the no cycle case
- Continuing to move fast by two after meeting is incorrect
hints: Reset one pointer to head after meeting
Advance both one step to find entry
